results.title: The best (actually free to use) AI tools for day-to-day work ...
results.content: without plugins, it has access to the internet. It also doesn't require signing in, which is nice. At the risk of sounding like a broken record, Google has really dropped the ball lately in delivering non-spammy search results that actually answer the query, and it's nice to see other search engines like Bing and Phind providing alternatives. Key Features: Similar to Phind, though arguably a bit better for non-technical questions: Bing similarly provides sourced summaries, generates content and otherwise integrates AI and search nicely. Built on top of GPT-4: like Taskade, Bing has confirmed they use GPT-4. That makes it another nice option to get around paying for GPT-4 while still getting much of the same capabilities as ChatGPT. Seamless integration with a standard search engine that’s [...] using natural language. It’s all fairly intuitive and the best of the AI slide tools I’ve tried. FlowGPT : Database of AI prompts and workflows (free without signup-though it pushes you to signup!) FlowGPT collects prompts and collections of prompts to do various tasks, from marketing, productivity, and coding to random stuff people find interesting. It uses an upvote system similar to Reddit that makes it easy to find interesting ways to use ChatGPT. It also lets you search for prompts if you have something in mind and want to see what others have done. It's free and has a lot of cool features like showing you previews of how ChatGPT responds to the prompts. Unfortunately, it's also a bit pushy with getting you to signup, and the design leaves something to be desired, but it's the best [...] built for website marketing/blog content. I've tried both but don't use them enough regularly to be able to recommend one over the other. Worth trying if you do a lot of content writing and want to automate parts of it. Let me know if you guys recommend any other free AI tools that you use day-to-day and I can add them to the list. I’m also interested in any requests you guys have for AI tools that don’t exist yet, as I’m looking for new projects to work on at the moment! TL;DR: ChatPDF : Interact with any PDF using ChatGPT without signing up, great for students and anyone who needs to filter through long PDFs. Taskade : All-in-one task management, scheduling, and notetaking with built-in GPT-4 Chat + AI assistant for improving productivity. Plus AI for Google Slides : Addon for Google

results.title: The 20+ best free AI tools in 2026
results.content:
Comet will attempt almost anything you'd normally do in a browser, with sensible guardrails around security and payments. In that Airbnb example, it will stop at the login screen and ask you to authenticate instead of touching your password manager. I also like that you can watch everything it does in real time and pause or step in if something doesn't feel right. It's perfect for small but annoying tasks you usually avoid, like grouping dozens of open tabs or tracking down a random link from weeks ago. [...] Comet's AI is always working in the background, not just when you ask a question. It learns how you browse and adapts in subtle ways (which, if you can get past the creepiness, is pretty useful). Tabs you're unlikely to revisit get deprioritized. Pages you tend to open at the same time each day load before you even click.

The Comet Assistant feature is a mix of a chatbot and an agent that can actually take action in your browser. Say you want to ask an Airbnb host whether the room has an office chair. Instead of opening the site yourself, you can ask Comet to handle it. It will navigate to Airbnb, open the message thread, draft a clear (and polite) note, and send it all on its own. [...] ChatGPT is the original AI chatbot, and it's still the industry leader, with hundreds of millions of weekly active users. With new models constantly being released, free users get access to powerful, modern versions of ChatGPT for everyday tasks, including writing, reasoning, file uploads, and limited image generation. You can also use custom-built GPTs created by other users to handle specific workflows. An upgrade to ChatGPT Plus is required to get things like higher limits, access to newer models, custom GPT creation, advanced voice features, and more powerful tools for research and agent-style workflows. As with most ChatGPT features, newer tools often roll out to paid plans first before gradually becoming available to free users.

results.title: The 7 Best Free AI Tools for Your Everyday Work
results.content:
### The most powerful all-in-one AI tool?

Manus AI is a powerful AI platform that handles complex tasks independently by breaking them down into subtasks, prioritizing them and processing them according to the “think-plan-execute” principle – including planning, execution and checking results, all without constant user interaction.

Thanks to a multi-agent and multi-model architecture, Manus combines the strengths of various specialized AI models such as GPT-4, Claude 3 or Qwen to efficiently solve a variety of tasks such as research, logical thinking or programming.

In addition, Manus analyzes and structures data from various sources, creates reports, carries out market or SEO analyses and provides interactive dashboards if required. [...] ### Areas of application of Manus AI:

## 4. Replit AI

Replit is a cloud-based development environment (IDE) that allows you to program directly in your browser – without having to install any development software locally. The name stands for “Read-Eval-Print Loop”, a term from the programming of interactive languages such as Python or JavaScript.

This AI tool is aimed at beginners, advanced and professional developers. The platform lowers the entry barrier for software development, accelerates prototyping and makes collaborative teamwork particularly easy.

Youtube Thumbnail

### Areas of application of Replit:

## 5. n8n [...] ## 1. ChatGPT

Almost everyone has heard of it these days: ChatGPT. The global hype surrounding chat-based AI is in full swing. This is precisely why the tool from OpenAI has earned the top spot on our list.

ChatGPT is an AI-based text platform based on the state-of-the-art GPT-4 architecture. Trained with billions of parameters, ChatGPT creates impressively detailed and high-quality texts that were hardly possible before. Especially for complex tasks, you get precise answers quickly and easily.
